QSC Sleestack Core Health Analysis Report

Observations:

  • runtime_engine accounts for 94.3% of total memory consumption
  • Two process cohorts: younger group (audio_engine, runtime_engine ~4.15 days), older group (node, video_engines, engine_discover ~5.94 days)
  • Thread allocation stable with no exhaustion signals

6. MEMORY LEAK DETECTION

⚠️ CRITICAL MEMORY LEAK DETECTED

ProcessGrowth Rate24h GrowthSeverity
runtime_engine (PID 5629)51.3 MB/hour+1.23 GB (+25.1%)CRITICAL
node (PID 9617)~0.17 MB/hour+4 MB (+0.6%)MINOR

Analysis:

  • runtime_engine shows uncontrolled memory accumulation: 4.91GB → 6.14GB over 24 hours
  • Monotonic growth pattern across all time buckets with no plateau
  • Leak will exhaust available memory if pattern continues unchecked
  • Four of six processes show perfect memory stability (0% variance), isolating issue to runtime_engine
  • Projected exhaustion: Depends on total system memory, but current trajectory is unsustainable

7. CRASH AND STACKTRACE ANALYSIS

✓ ZERO CRASHES DETECTED

Analysis:

  • No fatal errors logged to {service_name="stacktrace", instance="eternia-Core24-d447"} over 24-hour period
  • All process uptime metrics show continuous monotonic growth with no resets
  • No evidence of watchdog resets, kill signals, or forced restarts
  • Device has been running stably without interruption

8. METRICS AND LOG CORRELATION

Correlation Window: 2026-06-13 22:00 UTC to 2026-06-14 22:00 UTC

Key Insights:

  • Memory leak in runtime_engine occurs without corresponding CPU spikes or error logs
  • Silent leak pattern suggests gradual accumulation rather than crash-induced growth
  • No correlation between CPU peaks and memory growth events
  • Absence of stacktrace errors indicates memory growth is not triggering fatal exceptions (yet)

9. UPTIME AND RESTART ANALYSIS

Overall Uptime Stability: HEALTHY

Process GroupUptimeLast RestartStatus
Older Group
(engine_discover, node, video_engines)		5.94 days~6 days agoSTABLE

(audio_engine, runtime_engine)		4.15 days~4 days agoSTABLE

Analysis:

  • Zero restart events during 24-hour observation window
  • Two distinct process cohorts suggest planned maintenance or selective restart 1.79 days apart
  • All processes maintain single consistent PID (no respawns detected)
  • No partial crashes, emergency restarts, or system-wide failures
  • Monitoring gaps: 0% across all series

10. ANOMALIES

Anomaly TypeDetailsSeverity
Memory Leakruntime_engine growing at 51.3 MB/hour (1.23GB in 24h)CRITICAL
Inactive Processvideo_engine (PID 10439) completely inactive with 0% CPUWARNING
CPU Dominanceaudio_engine consuming 87% of total CPU loadWARNING

No anomalies detected in:

  • Thread exhaustion (all stable)
  • I/O wait contention (zero across all processes)
  • Temporal spikes or synchronized load events
  • Crash patterns or restart cycles

11. SUMMARY AND RISK ASSESSMENT

OVERALL STATUS: CRITICAL

ComponentRatingJustification
CPU HealthWARNINGaudio_engine high but stable load (31.81% avg)
Memory HealthCRITICALruntime_engine leak growing 51.3MB/hr, +25% in 24h
Process StabilityHEALTHYZero crashes, restarts, or errors over 24h
Thread ManagementHEALTHYAll processes stable, no exhaustion signals
System UptimeHEALTHYContinuous operation, no forced restarts

Risk Summary:

  • Immediate Risk: runtime_engine memory leak will eventually exhaust system memory and force OOM kill or system crash
  • Timeline: At current growth rate (51.3MB/hr), projected exhaustion depends on total available memory
  • Mitigation Status: No automatic recovery mechanism detected; manual intervention required
  • Positive Indicators: Zero crashes/errors suggest core is functionally stable despite memory leak; leak has not yet triggered fatal conditions

12. RECOMMENDED ACTIONS

IMMEDIATE (Within 24 hours):

  1. Restart runtime_engine process to reclaim 6.14GB leaked memory and prevent imminent OOM condition
  2. Enable memory profiling on runtime_engine (PID 5629) to identify leak source
  3. Review runtime_engine logs for clues about memory accumulation pattern
  4. Set up memory threshold alerts for runtime_engine (trigger at 50% utilization or 8GB absolute)

SHORT-TERM (Within 1 week):

  1. Investigate video_engine (PID 10439) inactivity — confirm if process should be running or can be terminated
  2. Deploy memory leak fix for runtime_engine once root cause identified via profiling
  3. Implement automated restart policy for runtime_engine as temporary mitigation (e.g., daily restart until fix deployed)
  4. Review audio_engine CPU load — 31.81% is high but stable; validate if optimization opportunities exist

LONG-TERM (Within 1 month):

  1. Implement comprehensive memory monitoring with anomaly detection for all processes
  2. Review process restart coordination — understand 1.79-day gap between cohort restarts
  3. Performance baseline documentation — establish normal operating ranges for CPU, memory, threads per process
  4. Capacity planning — determine if audio_engine CPU load will scale with increased workload

Report generated by QSC Sleestack Core Health Analysis Agent | Data from Prometheus (grafanacloud-prom) and Loki (grafanacloud-logs)
